user_query:
Please process a small UK VAT and ID validation batch for our March test run.

1) Read input/invoices.csv (columns: invoice_id, type, amount, rate_type, description). For each row:
   - If type is "net", compute VAT on the net amount using the rate_type (standard = 20%, reduced = 5%, zero = 0%).
   - If type is "gross", reverse-calculate to net and VAT using the rate_type (i.e., remove VAT from the gross amount).
   - Output one consolidated file at output/vat/line_items.json with an array of items. Each item must include: invoice_id, rate_type, net_gbp, vat_gbp, total_gbp. All GBP amounts must be strings formatted with a leading pound sign and two decimals (e.g., "£1,234.56").

2) Create a roll-up at output/vat/summary.json that includes totals grouped by rate_type (standard, reduced, zero), each with: invoice_count, net_total_gbp, vat_total_gbp, gross_total_gbp. Also include overall totals across all invoices and a checksum field equal to the sum of all VAT amounts (rounded to 2 decimals). Ensure all monetary fields are formatted as strings with a leading pound sign and two decimals.

3) Validate IDs from input/ids.yaml. Apply:
   - VAT number validity: must be "GB" followed by 9 digits and pass a modulus 97 check on the 9-digit number.
   - UTR validity: each UTR must be exactly 10 digits.
   - NINO validity: 2 letters + 6 digits + a suffix A-D; the first letter cannot be D, F, I, Q, U, or V.
   Write results to output/validations.json with keys: vat_number_valid (boolean), utrs_valid (object mapping each input UTR to a boolean), ninos_valid (object mapping each input NINO to a boolean).

4) Write a concise compliance note at output/notes.md that explains your assumptions (e.g., rounding method, handling of gross vs net), calls out edge cases (e.g., mixed-rated supplies, exempt items, credit notes), and makes at least one recommendation for review. Keep it under 400 words.

Create any missing folders under output/ as needed. Do not call any external services. Use only the provided inputs under input/ and write artifacts strictly under output/ at the specified paths.
